text: Resilience is the ability to adapt positively to adversity, trauma, or significant stress. Not a fixed trait — can be developed through practice. Key components: emotional regulation, optimism, cognitive flexibility, social support, sense of purpose

Growth mindset (Carol Dweck, 2006): belief that abilities can be developed through effort. Fixed mindset: belief abilities are innate. Students taught growth mindset show improved academic performance (Blackwell et al., 2007, effect size d=0.27-0.53)
